nodes.label: legend | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
The Creation Story – Turtle Island

For the Ojibway/Anishinabe people, the legend is as follows.

nodes.label: ∅ | people | earth
nodes.sentence:
Long ago, after the Great
Mystery, or Kitchi-Manitou, first peopled the earth, the Anishinabe, or Original People,
strayed from their
harmonious ways and began to argue and fight with one another.

nodes.label: brother | turn | brother
nodes.sentence:
Brother turned
against brother and soon the Anishinabe were killing one another over hunting grounds
and others disagreements.

nodes.label: manitou | decide | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Seeing that harmony, brotherhood, sisterhood, and respect
for all living things no longer prevailed on Earth, Kitchi-Manitou decided to purify the
Earth.

nodes.label: he | do | this
nodes.sentence: He did this with water.

nodes.label: water | come | form
nodes.sentence:
The water came in the form of a great flood, or mush-ko'-be-wun', upon the Earth
destroying the Anishinabe people and most of the animals as well.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Only Nanaboozhoo,
the central figure in many of the Anishinabe oral traditions, was able to survive the
flood, along with a few animals and birds who managed to swim and fly.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | allow | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Nanaboozhoo allowed the remaining animals and birds to
take turns resting on the log as well.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | speak | ∅
nodes.sentence: Finally, Nanaboozhoo spoke.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"I am going to do
something," he said.

nodes.label: i | go | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"I am going to swim to the bottom of this water and grab a handful
of earth.

nodes.label: i | believe | bit
nodes.sentence:
With this small bit of Earth, I believe we can create a new land for us to live on
with the help of the Four Winds and Kitchi-Manitou.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | dive | water
nodes.sentence:
"
So Nanaboozhoo dived into the water and was gone for a long time.

nodes.label: he | surface | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Finally he surfaced,
and short of breath told the animals that the water is too deep for him to swim to the
bottom.

nodes.label: all | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: All were silent.

nodes.label: mahng | speak | ∅
nodes.sentence: Finally, Mahng, the Loon spoke up.

nodes.label: that | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"I can dive under the water
for a long way, that is how I catch my food.

nodes.label: i | try | ∅
nodes.sentence:
I will try to make it to the bottom and return
with some Earth in my beak."

nodes.label: loon | disappear | ∅
nodes.sentence: The Loon disappeared and was gone for a very long time.

nodes.label: loon | drown | ∅
nodes.sentence: Surely, thought the others, the Loon must have drowned.

nodes.label: they | see | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Then they saw him float to the
surface, weak and nearly unconscious.

nodes.label: he | gasp | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"I couldn't make it, there must be no bottom to
this water," he gasped.

nodes.label: everyone | know | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Then Zhing-gi-biss, the helldiver came forward and said "I will try
next, everyone knows I can dive great distances."

nodes.label: helldiver | go | ∅
nodes.sentence: So the helldiver went under.

nodes.label: time | pass | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Again, a
very long time passed and the others thought he was surely drowned.

nodes.label: he | float | last
nodes.sentence:
At last he too
floated to the surface.

nodes.label: he | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
He was unconscious, and not till he came to could he relate to
the others that he too was unable to fetch the Earth from the bottom.

nodes.label: animal | try | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Many more animals tried but failed, including Zhon-gwayzh', the mink, and even Mi-
zhee-kay", the turtle.

nodes.label: all | fail | ∅
nodes.sentence:
All failed and it seemed as though there was no way to get the
much needed Earth from the bottom.

nodes.label: voice | hear | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then a soft muffled voice was heard.

nodes.label: it | speak | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"I can do it,"
it spoke softly.

nodes.label: one | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: At first no one could see who it was that spoke up.

nodes.label: zhushk | step | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Then, the little Wa-
zhushk", muskrat stepped forward.

nodes.label: he | repeat | ∅
nodes.sentence: "I'll try," he repeated.

nodes.label: some | laugh | muskrat
nodes.sentence:
Some of the other, bigger,
more powerful animals laughed at muskrat.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | speak | ∅
nodes.sentence: Nanaboozhoo spoke up.

nodes.label: manitou | place | judgment
nodes.sentence:
"Only Kitchi-
Manitou can place judgment on others.

nodes.label: he | allow | ∅
nodes.sentence:
If muskrat wants to try, he should be allowed
to."

nodes.label: muskrat | dive | water
nodes.sentence: So, muskrat dove into the water.

nodes.label: he | go | ∅
nodes.sentence:
He was gone much longer than any of the others
who tried to reach the bottom.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | be | while
nodes.sentence:
After a while Nanaboozhoo and the other animals were
certain that muskrat had give his life trying to reach the bottom.

nodes.label: ∅ | reach | bottom
nodes.sentence: Far below the water's surface, muskrat, had in fact reached the bottom.

nodes.label: he | grab | earth
nodes.sentence:
Very weak from
lack of air, he grabbed some Earth in his paw and with all the energy he could muster
began to swim for the surface.

nodes.label: one | spot | muskrat
nodes.sentence:
One of the animals spotted muskrat as he floated to the
surface.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | pull | he
nodes.sentence: Nanaboozhoo pulled him up onto the log.

nodes.label: he | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
"Brothers and sisters," Nanaboozhoo
said, “muskrat went too long without air, he is dead."

nodes.label: song | hear | water
nodes.sentence:
A song of mourning and praise
was heard across the water as muskrat's spirit passed on to the spirit world.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | something
nodes.sentence:
Suddenly
Nanaboozhoo exclaimed, "Look, there is something in his paw!"

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | open | paw
nodes.sentence:
Nanaboozhoo carefully
opened the tiny paw.

nodes.label: animal | gather | ∅
nodes.sentence:
All the animals gathered
close to see what was held so tightly there.

nodes.label: paw | open | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Muskrat's paw opened and revealed a small
ball of
Earth.

nodes.label: animal | shout | joy
nodes.sentence: The animals all shouted with joy.

nodes.label: muskrat | sacrifice | life
nodes.sentence:
Muskrat sacrificed his life so that life on Earth
could begin anew.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | take | piece
nodes.sentence: Nanaboozhoo took the piece of Earth from Muskrat's paw.

nodes.label: turtle | swam | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Just then, the turtle swam
forward and said, "Use my back to bear the weight of this piece of Earth.

nodes.label: we | make | earth
nodes.sentence:
With the help
of Kitchi-Manitou, we can make a new Earth."

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | put | piece
nodes.sentence:
Nanaboozhoo put the piece of Earth on
the turtle's back.

nodes.label: wind | blow | each
nodes.sentence:
Suddenly, the wind blew from each of the Four Directions, The tiny
piece of Earth on the turtle's back began to grow.

nodes.label: it | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence:
It grew and grew and grew until it
formed a mi- ni-si', or island in the water.

nodes.label: island | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence:
The island grew larger and larger, but still the
turtle bore the weight of the Earth on his back.

nodes.label: nanaboozhoo | sing | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Nanaboozhoo and the animals all sang
and danced in a widening circle on the growing island.

nodes.label: wind | cease | while
nodes.sentence:
After a while, the Four Winds
ceased to blow and the waters became still.

nodes.label: island | sit | middle
nodes.sentence:
A huge island sat in the middle of the water,
and today that island is known as North America.

nodes.label: people | hold | reverence
nodes.sentence:
Traditional Indian people, including the Ojibway, hold special reverence for the turtle
who sacrificed his life and made life possible for the Earth's second people.

nodes.label: muskrat | give | life
nodes.sentence:
To this day,
the muskrat has been given a good life.

nodes.label: muskrat | continue | ∅
nodes.sentence:
No matter that marshes have been drained and
their homes destroyed in the name of progress, the muskrat continues to survive and
multiply.

nodes.label: they | build | home
nodes.sentence:
The muskrats do their part today in remembering the great flood; they build
their homes in the shape of the little ball of Earth and the island that was formed from
it.
